items.title: Teenage girl reported missing while hiking in Tanjong Malim, search underway
items.summary: IPOH, May 24 — A teenage girl has been reported missing after possibly being lost while hiking at Bukit Changkat Asa near Tanjong Malim yesterday. Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said they received a call at 1.26 pm and dispatched a team of firefighters to the location. The search and rescue operation was suspended at 7 pm yesterday and would be handed over to the Kuala Kubu Bharu Fire and Rescue Department for further action, he added.

items.title: MB: Johor declares Monday special holiday after JDT win Malaysia Cup
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 24 — The Johor state government has declared a special state holiday on Monday following Johor Darul Ta’zim’s (JDT) Malaysia Cup win last night. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the declaration was made with the consent of Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail in appreciation to the Johor people and Southern Tiger fans for their undivided support to the team. The success proved the continued commitment of the Johor leadership in developing modern high performance football that made JDT the state’s pride and a respected team at the national and Asian level, he added.

items.title: Man found dead inside car had past record of 20 criminal offences
items.summary: BALING: Police have confirmed that the body found inside a car by the roadside here yesterday was that of a local man with a prior record involving 20 general and drug-related cases. District police chief Superintendent Brandon Richard Joe also said checks revealed that the car had been reported stolen in Pendang. Upon arriving at the scene, police found the car locked, and the man inside was confirmed dead.

items.title: King congratulates JDT on historic fourth straight Malaysia Cup win
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has extended his congratulations to the Johor Darul Ta’zim Football Club (JDT) on their success in capturing their sixth Malaysia Cup title yesterday. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, His Majesty also expressed his appreciation for the fighting spirit shown by the players and coaching staff, as well as the unwavering support of the loyal JDT fans who stand by the team through thick and thin. Sultan Ibrahim also extended his congratulations to his son, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, who is also the owner of JDT.

items.title: Anwar takes over Pandan as adopted constituency after Rafizi’s exit, Tengku Zafrul named monitoring officer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) president, has taken over Pandan as his adopted parliamentary constituency following the departure of its former MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli from the party. PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said Prime Minister’s senior political adviser Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz had meanwhile been appointed as the party’s monitoring officer for the constituency. “Tengku Zafrul has also been entrusted to monitor the Ampang parliamentary constituency.

items.title: US, Mexico border agencies coordinate ahead of World Cup
items.summary: SAN DIEGO: United States and Mexican border authorities are working together to prepare for increased cross-border movement during the 2026 Fifa World Cup. US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Director of Field Operations for San Diego, Sidney Aki, said the agency was already working with the Mexican government to manage movement through ports of entry. Aki said San Diego was expected to see some of that movement, especially as travellers move between the US and Mexico for matches or related activities.

items.title: Police declare bomb threat at prominent KL building a hoax after sweep, probe underway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Police have confirmed that a bomb threat made against a prominent building in the capital last night was false. Brickfields district police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said police received a call regarding the incident at 9.09 pm from a security guard at the building. “The building’s security guard informed us that he had received an email sent to the customer service centre stating that an individual had placed a bomb on the 23rd floor of the building’s north tower,” he said in a statement.

items.title: #NSTviral: Heartwarming rescue of monkey on rain-lashed highway goes viral [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A touching video documenting the rescue of a dusky leaf monkey found stranded in the middle of a busy highway in Kulai, Johor during a heavy downpour has gone viral. The story gained traction after veterinarian Dr Marlia Zulkapli shared the journey of the monkey, which was discovered on the road in torrential rain. Dr Marlia said that the traffic seemed to part naturally to allow the rescue to take place safely.

items.title: No restrictions yet on non-Islamic houses of worship in Selangor commercial areas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Selangor government has not enforced or implemented newly drawn-up planning guidelines that bar non-Islamic houses of worship from being built in commercial areas. Ng was referring to the recently published Selangor State Planning Guidelines and Standards for Community Facilities, which are administered by the state town and country planning department, PLANMalaysia Selangor. "The state government wishes to address recent concerns regarding PLANMalaysia Selangor's guidelines on non-Muslim places of worship, specifically concerning the approval of future applications for non-Muslim places of worship in commercial shoplots.

items.title: Barrier-free toll goal could hit speed bump, warn experts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Widespread toll evasion could undermine Malaysia's ambitions to modernise its highway network through the Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) toll collection system, experts said. They said weak enforcement and poor motorist compliance may threaten the success of the system. She said toll evasion offences continued despite enforcement and monitoring measures.

items.title: Perak PKR wants Anwar to stay in Tambun for GE16
items.summary: IPOH: Perak PKR wants Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to remain in Tambun for the next general election to strengthen the party's election campaign in the state. "We in Perak certainly need the president to remain here to help us win more parliamentary and state seats in the next election," he told reporters after the opening of the new Perak PKR headquarters in Meru here yesterday. Also present was Perak PKR deputy chairman Tan Kar Hing.

items.title: HR Ministry highlights reforms, benefits and protections for migrant workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry is refining reforms to address the root causes of mental health issues among migrant workers, particularly labour rights abuses and financial pressures. The ministry said there is a direct link between mental health and labour exploitation, citing the International Labour Organisation's (ILO) recognition of the "abuse of vulnerability" as a key indicator of forced labour. Acknowledging that mental health issues among migrant workers are severely underreported, it said figures recorded by the Occupational Safety and Health Department do not reflect reality.

items.title: Another Malaysian haj pilgrim dies in Holy Land, bringing total to nine
items.summary: MAKKAH: A 77‑year‑old Malaysian male haj pilgrim died here on Thursday due to intra‑abdominal sepsis. In a statement, Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) said the pilgrim, from Machang, Kelantan, was pronounced dead at King Abdul Aziz Hospital at 5.40pm. TH also advised haj pilgrims to take care of their health, get sufficient rest and seek immediate treatment at TH healthcare centres if they experience any health problems while in the Holy Land.

items.title: Debt, abuse and isolation push domestic workers to the brink
items.summary: Suherman, a migrant worker and founding member of Serantau Malaysia, an organisation advocating for the welfare of Indonesian migrants, said domestic workers, most of whom are women, face intense pressure. Suherman said many distressed migrant workers share the same experience — being denied basic rights. Among migrant workers, Suherman said domestic workers are the most vulnerable.

items.title: Bomb threat at prominent KL building a hoax, police confirm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have confirmed that a bomb threat made against a prominent building in the capital last night was false. Brickfields district police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said police received a call at 9.09pm from a security guard at the building. According to him, a Bomb Disposal Unit, K9 Unit and Forensic Unit were deployed to the location to conduct a search, which confirmed the threat was false.

items.title: Mental health expert calls for systemic reforms for migrant workers' well-being
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An expert has called for culturally tailored helplines, protections against job loss or deportation for seeking help and mental health training to address a growing crisis among migrant workers. Malaysian Mental Health Association (MMHA) president Professor Datuk Dr Andrew Mohanraj also called for mandatory employer responsibilities ­— safe accommodation, reasonable working hours, guaranteed leave and access to medical care. Commenting on mental health issues affecting foreigners, particularly those from Nepal, Andrew said financial pressure, loneliness and exhaustion were key drivers of poor mental health and suicide.

items.title: Punishment for toll dodgers: Fines can deter offenders, jail may be too harsh [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: While criminalising toll evasion could be one way to deter offenders, Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said the government must strike a balance between effective punishment and fairness. Nanta said although fines could serve as a deterrent, some motorists had become accustomed to paying penalties without changing their behaviour. He said the government is working to resolve enforcement concerns ahead of the Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) toll collection rollout.

items.title: Large police presence around White House after reports of shots fired
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Police and security forces swarmed the White House on Saturday evening after shots were fired in the area, authorities said. Police cordoned off access to the White House and National Guard troops blocked an AFP reporter from entering the area in downtown Washington. "FBI is on the scene and supporting Secret Service responding to shots fired near White House grounds," FBI Director Kash Patel said on X. Canadian tourist Reid Adrian told AFP he was in the area when "we heard probably 20 to 25 what sounded like fireworks, but they're gunshots, and then everyone started running."

items.title: Isolation, mounting debts add to mental health issues among migrant workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chandra Mogar, 42, gives little away. Purna gave a small shrug. "He would say many things," Purna said.

items.title: Kyiv hit by massive ballistic missile barrage
items.summary: KYIV: A "mass ballistic missile attack" pounded Kyiv early Sunday, military authorities said, with AFP journalists hearing heavy explosions, after Russia threatened retaliation for Ukrainian strikes in the east. A residential building located close to the government district shook from the explosions and dozens of people took shelter in an underground metro station in central Kyiv, AFP reporters saw, as officials urged people to take cover. "The capital has come under a mass ballistic missile attack," Tymur Tkachenko, head of the Kyiv City Military Administration, wrote on Telegram.

items.title: Breaking point: The mental health crisis facing migrant workers [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In six years, 344 Nepali migrant workers have taken their own lives, raising serious concerns over foreign workers' mental health. Alarm bells were first raised in 2021 when the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) revealed suicide as a leading cause of death among Nepali migrants. Pusat Komas director and former Suhakam commissioner Jerald Joseph, who first highlighted the findings, said input from the Nepali embassy revealed pressures and tensions that drove workers to take their own lives.

items.title: High Court awards Nigerian RM675,000 over unlawful detention: A-GC mulls appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) is reviewing the High Court's decision which awarded a Nigerian man RM675,000 in damages after finding that he was unlawfully detained for about 40 days in 2021. The court assessed general damages at RM15,000 per day for wrongful detention, while exemplary damages were fixed at RM75,000. On April 23, 2021, then High Court judge Datuk Ab Karim Ab Rahman ordered Momoh's immediate release after allowing his habeas corpus application.

items.title: One shock away from crisis: Report says nearly a fifth of PPR households have no savings, over half can last only three months or less
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Public housing in Malaysia, originally designed as short-term shelter for the urban poor, now risks keeping its tenants for far longer than intended, according to a recent Think City study. The report found that almost three-quarters of some 3,000 People’s Housing Project (PPR) residents surveyed are vulnerable to economic shocks. The report, titled “From Roof to Resilience”, polled 2,884 people across 10 public housing communities in the Klang Valley.

items.title: Gaza flotilla activists return to Ireland, UK
items.summary: DUBLIN: British and Irish activists who were part of a Gaza-bound flotilla seized and deported by Israel returned to London and Dublin on Saturday, following global condemnation over their treatment. Among the Irish activists who arrived at Dublin Airport via Turkiye was Margaret Connolly, sister of Irish President Catherine Connolly. Seven UK-based activists also landed at London's Stansted Aiport on Saturday, many still wearing Israeli prison tracksuits.

items.title: Ebola death toll tops 200 as fears grow over spread across Africa
items.summary: ADDIS ABABA: Officials in the Democratic Republic of Congo updated the death toll from the Ebola outbreak to 204 late Saturday, hours after the Red Cross said three volunteers had died there and Uganda confirmed three new Ebola cases. A health ministry statement said 204 deaths had been recorded in three provinces of the vast central African country, from 867 suspected cases. On Saturday, the African Union's health agency warned that more countries on the continent were at risk of being affected by the Ebola virus, in addition to the DRC and Uganda.

items.title: Israeli strikes rock Lebanon as Hizbollah signals unwavering Iran support
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel struck south Lebanon on Saturday despite a ceasefire as Hizbollah said its backer Teheran had pledged not to abandon the group. Lebanon's state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported Israeli airstrikes on multiple south Lebanon locations, some after Israel issued evacuation warnings to around 15 villages. Lebanon's military said one strike targeted an army barracks in the city of Nabatieh, wounding a soldier.

items.title: Trump claims Iran deal largely negotiated, includes reopening Hormuz Strait
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Saturday a deal with Iran had been "largely negotiated," with the proposal including opening the crucial Strait of Hormuz, though the agreement was "subject to finalisation." Among the Middle East countries whose leaders joined a call on Saturday to discuss the deal were Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Egypt, Jordan and Bahrain, Trump said. Teheran said it was finalising a 14-point framework for a deal.

items.title: Anwar takes over Pandan as adopted constituency
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) president, has taken over Pandan as his adopted parliamentary constituency following the departure of its former MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli from the party. PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said Prime Minister's senior political adviser Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz had meanwhile been appointed as the party's monitoring officer for the constituency. "Tengku Zafrul has also been entrusted to monitor the Ampang parliamentary constituency.

items.title: [UPDATED] Johor declares Monday a special holiday after JDT's Malaysia Cup triumph
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor government has declared Monday (May 25) as a special public holiday in celebration of Johor Darul Ta'zim's (JDT) Malaysia Cup 2025/26 victory last night. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the declaration was made with the consent of the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail. "Upon the consent of His Royal Highness the Regent of Johor, the state government has agreed to declare Monday, May 25, 2026, as a special holiday for Johor in conjunction with JDT's Malaysia Cup 2025/26 triumph," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Wrong-way Bezza driver arrested; tests positive for drugs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a man in his late 30s over a viral video showing a car being driven against traffic along Jalan Hang Tuah here. In a statement, Cheras district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the suspect tested positive for amphetamine and methamphetamine following a urine test. Police are expected to seek a remand order today.

items.title: King congratulates JDT on winning 6th Malaysia Cup title
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has extended his congratulations to the Johor Darul Ta'zim Football Club (JDT) on their success in capturing their sixth Malaysia Cup title today. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, His Majesty also expressed his appreciation for the fighting spirit shown by the players and coaching staff, as well as the unwavering support of the loyal JDT fans who stand by the team through thick and thin. Sultan Ibrahim also extended his congratulations to his son, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, who is also the owner of JDT.

items.title: Bangladesh's rare 'Donald Trump' buffalo becomes Eid sensation
items.summary: DHAKA: A rare albino buffalo with flowing blond hair has become an unlikely celebrity in Bangladesh ahead of the Eid al-Adha festival, drawing crowds of curious visitors who say the animal bears a striking resemblance to US President Donald Trump. "My younger brother named the buffalo Donald Trump because the hair on the front of its head resembles Donald Trump," farm owner Ziauddin Mridha said. [[nid:1446719]] Unusual animals go viral during Eid festival Mridha said the buffalo required special care, including being bathed four times a day and fed four meals daily to keep it healthy and in good condition before Eid.

items.title: Iran, US finalising memorandum of understanding - Official
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran and Washington are at the final stage of work on a 14-point memorandum of understanding, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Ismail Baghaei said on Saturday, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. We are currently finalising this memorandum of understanding," Baghaei was quoted as saying by Iran's Fars news agency . Meanwhile, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Saturday announced progress on Iran and suggested there could be news soon.

items.title: JDT star Arif Aiman to end bachelorhood next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor Darul Ta'zim (JDT) sensation Arif Aiman Hanapi is confirmed to be ending his bachelorhood this Monday. The news was confirmed following the circulation of the national winger's wedding invitation, which has been widely shared across social media platforms. "The ceremony is expected to take place in a lively atmosphere with the presence of family members, close acquaintances, and several important figures in Malaysian football," Harian Metro reported.

items.title: Queen arrives in Turkiye for special visit
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, has arrived in Istanbul, Turkiye in conjunction with a three-day special visit to the country. Her Majesty was accompanied by the Tunku Temenggong of Johor Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim, Tunku Panglima of Johor Tunku Abdul Rahman Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim and Tunku Putera of Johor Tunku Abu Bakar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim. – Bernama TAGS: Raja Zarith Sofiah, Her Majesty, special visit, Turkiye, Royal Malaysian Navy, ship © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Pulau Banggi residents relieved as SARA aid now redeemable on the island, cutting costly trips to Kudat
items.summary: KUDAT, May 23 — Pulau Banggi residents can now breathe a sigh of relief as they no longer need to travel to mainland Kudat to redeem the RM100 Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) For All cash assistance, following the availability of the facility on the island since September last year. The Kampung Karakit resident said in the past, Pulau Banggi residents had to take a ferry to Kudat to redeem the aid, resulting in part of the assistance being spent on return fares. Idris said the ferry fare from Pulau Banggi to Kudat costs RM26 one way, bringing the total return trip to RM52, a significant amount for low-income island residents.

items.title: KL’s 119-year-old General Post Office reopens as cultural hub with dining, retail offerings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — The rejuvenated Old General Post Office (GPO) building in Kuala Lumpur officially reopened today, marking a significant milestone in Khazanah Nasional’s Warisan KL project to conserve and redevelop culturally significant buildings in the capital. The newly renovated Old General Post Office on Jalan Raja, Kuala Lumpur, May 23, 2026. — Picture by Sayuti Zainudin Opened in 1907, the old General Post Office of Kuala Lumpur is among the city’s heritage landmarks.

items.title: KTMB boosts capacity to record 347,000 seats for Aidiladha-Wesak long break, largest in company’s history
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) is providing 347,000 seats for the upcoming long holiday period, marking the largest capacity increase in the company’s history. KTMB Group chief executive officer Datuk Azlan Shah Al Bakri said the capacity provided for the festive season has surpassed the company’s previous records for train services. Azlan Shah said that of the tickets covering more than 900 train trips, nearly 80 per cent or 270,000 tickets have already been sold.

items.title: PAS set for crunch talks on Bersatu cooperation ahead of Aidiladha, says party sec-gen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PAS is expected to hold a meeting within the next few days to decide its next course of action regarding ties with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu), following growing tensions within Perikatan Nasional (PN). “Whether we meet among ourselves first (PAS), then Bersatu, we will see. Hadi said several recent moves by Bersatu had caused unease within PAS.

items.title: Abang Johari likens Sarawak’s economic ambition to Bavaria, calls state Malaysia’s heartbeat
items.summary: BINTULU, May 23 — Unity among Sarawak’s multiracial and multi-religious communities remains the cornerstone of the state’s success in positioning itself as Malaysia’s emerging economic powerhouse, said Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. According to a Sarawak Public Communications Unit (Ukas) report, Abang Johari said Sarawak’s political stability and strong social cohesion have enabled the state government to aggressively pursue future industries such as green energy, hydrogen and the low-carbon economy. Abang Johari said Sarawak’s bold but carefully calculated move into the green energy sector is now yielding results, with the state becoming the first hydrogen production hub in the Asean region.

items.title: KPDN: Festive price control for Kaamatan, Gawai runs May 27–June 4 in Sabah, Sarawak, Labuan
items.summary: KUNDASANG, May 23 — The Festive Season Maximum Price Scheme (SHMMP) of the Kaamatan Festival and Gawai Day on May 30, 31 and June 1, respectively, will be implemented from May 27 to June 4 and involve 17 controlled items in Sabah, Sarawak and FT Labuan. Armizan said the Ministry has increased the number of controlled goods to 12, this year, from 10 last year, with imported old ginger and potatoes from China added to the list. For Labuan, 11 items will be controlled, excluding live pigs.

items.title: Honda HR-V driver accused of road rage after red-light crash into Perodua Bezza in viral PJ incident
items.summary: The 12.27am incident along Jalan PJU 8/1 involved a Honda HR-V and a Perodua Bezza. Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the suspect, a man in his 20s, is believed to have ignored a red traffic light before crashing into the Bezza. “The suspect was detained at the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters for reckless driving,” Shamsudin said in a statement today.

items.title: Agriculture Ministry: No reports of food shortages, supply sufficient until June despite global crisis
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 23 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (KPKM) said it has, to date, not received any reports of food supply shortages, including during the festive season, amid the global supply crisis. KPKM deputy secretary-general (Development) Datuk Luqman Ahmad said that simulations conducted, taking into account unpredictable weather conditions, indicated that the country’s food supply would remain sufficient until June. Meanwhile, Luqman said the Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority’s (Fama) Agro Madani Sales recorded encouraging performance, with sales amounting to RM101 million last year through 4,695 programmes held nationwide, benefiting over 6.89 million consumers.

items.title: Socso: 46,542 commuting accidents last year, most fatalities among breadwinners
items.summary: Perkeso chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the statistics reflected an alarming trend, particularly among motorcyclists, adding that most fatalities involved family breadwinners. He said Perkeso, in collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police’s Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT), launched the MyRider campaign to strengthen efforts to prevent road accidents, especially those involving motorcyclists. Dignified’,” he said.

items.title: Decomposed body found in locked Proton Wira parked for days in Baling
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Firefighters were called to Jalan Jerat Cina in Tawar, Baling, today to force open a locked Proton Wira after a person was found deceased inside. According to Berita Harian , Baling Fire and Rescue Station chief Assistant Fire Superintendent Zulkhairi Mat Tanjil said firefighters received a request for special assistance at about 12.15pm. “The operation involved using special equipment to break the car window and unlock the vehicle,” he said.

items.title: Woman fights off robbery attempt at Bukit Jalil hospital car park, repeat offender jailed for 11 months
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — A man who attempted to rob a woman in the parking area of Columbia Asia Hospital Bukit Jalil has been jailed for 11 months after pleading guilty to the offence, police said today. Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the 34-year-old local man was sentenced yesterday under Section 393 of the Penal Code for attempted robbery. No material losses were reported,” Mohd Rosdi said in a statement.

items.title: Remain calm and focus on ‘rakyat’, Perlis Perikatan chief says amid state assembly dissolution talk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) leaders should remain calm following Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah’s statement that he is prepared to dissolve the state assembly if PAS lawmakers move a vote of no confidence against him, Datuk Seri Shahidan Kassim said today. According to Utusan Malaysia , the Perlis PN chairman said all parties should wait for an official decision on cooperation between PAS and Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu), amid growing tensions within the Opposition coalition. The conclusion is simple — stay calm,” he told the national daily after officiating the 2026 National Youth Day celebration in Kuala Perlis today.

items.title: Lorry driver detained over alleged use of forged permits to buy subsidised petrol in Terengganu
items.summary: BANDAR PERMAISURI, May 23 — A lorry driver has been detained on suspicion of using forged permits to purchase subsidised petrol for sale to industrial operators. Terengganu Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) director Mohd Mufsi Lat said the local man, in his 30s, was detained during an Ops Tiris 4.0 raid conducted at a petrol station in Merang near here at 7.30 am today. He said two drums containing 500 litres of subsidised petrol worth approximately RM1,600 were also seized during the operation.

items.title: Aidiladha getaway traffic crawls on Karak, PLUS highways as LLM warns of worse jams ahead
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Traffic was building up across several major highways by midday today as motorists began travelling ahead of the Hari Raya Aidiladha holidays, according to the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM). According to Berita Harian, LLM senior assistant director of operations and maintenance Ahmad Kamil Abdul Halim said congestion was already forming along the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK), particularly from the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) to the Gombak toll plaza, with heavier traffic continuing towards Genting Sempah at KM30.5. LLM said 2.9 million vehicles used highways nationwide yesterday as holiday traffic began building up.

items.title: Malaysian youths are judging governments by delivery, not drama, says minister
items.summary: KANGAR, May 23 — Malaysian youth today are becoming more mature and wiser in evaluating the effectiveness of a government based on its performance and the country’s achievements, said Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari. He said the acceptance by the young generation of the current unity government has been positive, including among rural youth who are increasingly aware of current issues and national development. According to him, young people today no longer focus solely on political rhetoric, but instead assess the government based on economic performance, employment opportunities and national stability.

items.title: Anwar applauds Serdang Hospital team for world-class, life-saving cardiac care
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today congratulated the staff of Sultan Idris Shah Hospital (HSIS), particularly the Heart Centre team, for their outstanding achievements in delivering high-quality healthcare services to the public. In a Facebook post today, Anwar said the hospital’s ability to manage about 60 heart patients daily, including critical cases, reflected world-class expertise, efficiency and a high level of preparedness among the country’s healthcare personnel. Hospital Serdang was officially rebranded as HSIS on May 8, 2023.

items.title: Fowl play: 20kg python caught lurking inside Tuaran chicken coop
items.summary: TUARAN, May 23 — A 20-kilogram reticulated python was captured by the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) Tuaran district after it was found coiled inside a chicken coop at Taman Jelita 2 early Friday morning. Tuaran District and Tamparuli Unit APM officer Nur Azierah Alang said the team received an emergency call from a member of the public at 6.31am before deploying personnel to the location. “Fearing for the safety of family members, the complainant immediately contacted the Tuaran District Operations Control Centre (PKOD) for assistance,” she said in a statement.

items.title: Prime Minister Anwar calls for outreach to hawkers and SMEs still unaware of government loan facilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged the relevant agencies to intensify ground efforts to provide explanations and assistance to small and medium traders who are still unaware of financing facilities and aid provided by the government. He said hawkers and small traders should continue to be given attention, as they number hundreds of thousands but still lack a strong platform to voice their views. Regarding his visit, Anwar said the feedback he received from hawkers and small traders was generally very positive, with most expressing gratitude for the opportunity to obtain micro-financing facilities provided by the government and its agencies.

items.title: Rafizi-linked Bersama forms Johor pro-tem committee, eyes all 56 seats in state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 22 — Johor has become the first state to set up a pro-tem committee for the Malaysian United Party (Bersama) nationwide ahead of the coming state election. He said Johor’s membership currently consists of about 30 per cent former PKR members, with the majority comprising veteran and founding members. “The Johor Bersama committee members consist of former PKR state and division leaders, including a former state assemblyman.

items.title: Sabah maps out 25-year forest plan, pledges to retain 50pc forest cover
items.summary: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the state government, through the Sabah Forestry Department, is drafting a Statewide Forest Master Plan that will serve as a guideline for managing Sabah’s forest resources over the next 25 years. Hajiji said Sabah currently maintains 63 per cent forest cover, comprising 3.85 million hectares gazetted as forest reserves and other protected areas. At the same time, Hajiji said Sabah has identified about 400,000 hectares of degraded forest reserve land for forest plantation development under the Forest Plantation Development Action Plan 2022–2036.

items.title: Toyota Hilux driver allegedly high on ‘mushroom vape’ crashes into pedestrian and shopfront in Kuantan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — A 21-year-old driver high on a suspected “mushroom vape” ploughed into a pedestrian and smashed into a shopfront in Kuantan yesterday, police said. According to Utusan Malaysia , Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said the suspect had been driving a Toyota Hilux from Jalan Teluk Sisek towards the city centre before losing control outside a hardware store. Police later searched the vehicle and reportedly found a plastic bottle containing synthetic MDMA.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of severe weather in six states, including Kota Kinabalu and Johor Bahru
items.summary: In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the affected areas on the East Coast are Kuala Terengganu and Marang in Terengganu, as well as Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin in Pahang. In Johor, the affected areas are Tangkak, Segamat, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru. Meanwhile, in Sabah, the warning covers the Interior areas of Sipitang, Tenom, Beaufort, Keningau and Tambunan, as well as the West Coast areas of Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud.

items.title: No mass exodus from PKR, says Youth chief after Rafizi and Nik Nazmi departures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PKR’s Youth wing today dismissed talk of a mass exodus from the party following the departure of two of its senior leaders, insisting only a handful of members had left and that support among younger members remained strong. According to the New Straits Times , Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK) chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said the party had not seen any significant wave of resignations after former deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad quit PKR on May 17. Muhammad Kamil said PKR remained resilient despite the high-profile exits, pointing to the party’s survival through previous political crises.

items.title: Semporna PKR chief quits party after 27 years, cites shift from reformist ideals
items.summary: SEMPORNA, May 23 — Semporna PKR chief Mohd Amin Abdul Mem has resigned from all party posts and quit PKR after 27 years, citing a shift away from the party’s original reformist principles. “PKR today has slightly changed its rhythm. Mohd Amin expressed gratitude to party leaders and grassroots members before formally announcing his resignation and departure from PKR.
